biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — 7/11 non-trivial units (64%); unit 4 (ls-via-Bash x2 head/tail split, grep-rln-via-Bash, rg-via-Bash x5+, cat|grep pipeline on package.json, ls /docs/ x2 via Bash), unit 6 (grep-n via Bash for heading anchors, rg-via-Bash x2, wc-l-via-Bash), unit 8 (rg-via-Bash x4+, wc-l-via-Bash), unit 21 (rg-l-via-Bash, for-loop rg-via-Bash), unit 25 (ls-via-Bash), unit 32 (find-via-Bash x2, /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x8+), unit 34 (cat-n-via-Bash instead of Read, /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x4+, for-loop-Bash success=false); rate 64% is marginal improvement from 91% and 100% in prior two cycles but /usr/bin/grep explicit-path form structurally identical across all measured cycles with zero internalization

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:STABLE — unit 6: Read then 10+ Edit calls on plan.md with interleaved rg-via-Bash anchors and Reads; unit 8: Edit(success=false) then rg anchor then Read then 9+ Edit calls on plan.md with interleaved rg anchors; unit 32: 4 Edit calls on data-history-v3.tsx plus 6+ Edit calls on data-history-v3.table.tsx plus 3 Edit calls on s1-upload.tsx with interleaved Reads; peak Edit count per file (6+) unchanged from all prior measured cycles; no improvement trend across 4+ consecutive cycles

path_assumption_bias:RECURRING:STABLE — unit 4: Read on use-sync-q.ts (success=false) followed by ls-via-Bash pivot; same cross-session failure on this exact path persists; use-sync-q.ts has now produced Read(success=false) across 7+ consecutive sessions with zero cross-session internalization; no same-session repeat this cycle (consistent with prior cycle, an improvement over worst-case)

optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:STABLE — unit 8: Edit on plan.md (success=false) before rg anchor + Read + successful Edit; unit 34: Edit on file-select.v2.tsx (success=false) before grep anchor + successful Edit; two instances consistent with prior two cycles; no recovery trend
recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations rate improved marginally from 91-100% to 64% — /usr/bin/grep explicit-path and ls-via-Bash remain structurally unchanged; prior cycle recommendations have not been internalized; a PreToolUse hook blocking /usr/bin/grep explicit-path invocations would be more durable than continued flagging

path_assumption_bias on use-sync-q.ts has persisted 7+ consecutive sessions with zero cross-session internalization; write a persistent memory entry mapping the attempted path to the correct resolved location — this is the highest-ROI single fix available given frequency and zero improvement

re_edit_thrash root cause appears to be editing large plan.md and TSX files without mapping section offsets before editing; a mandatory pre-edit Read-and-outline step for files over 100 lines would address both re_edit_thrash and optimistic_edit_without_read simultaneously; these two biases have co-occurred in every cycle and share the same root
